aliases: Time Value of Money in Wealth Building
definition: Wealth compounds in the gap between willingness to wait and others' impatience; extreme time horizons enable exponential returns.
rationale: Cohesive concept about temporal arbitrage as driver of wealth; all members address same mechanism (patience → compounding) with different illustrative angles.

definition: Play to your existing competencies (skills, networks, experience) rather than chasing theoretically optimal channels.
rationale: Both members express identical principle: leverage what you already own (skillset, network) instead of inventing. Proposed canonical too narrow; broader concept about competitive advantage.

definition: Offer free access backed by card penalty if client doesn't execute; removes friction while triggering urgency, converting faster than friction-based sales.
rationale: All three frame the same offer shape: free access + contingent penalty. Mechanism is clear (urgency via penalty) and falsifiable (converts faster measurably).

splits.definition: Once you sell at premium price, client expectations and self-perception shift upward; price signals quality and anchors future negotiations.

definition: First high-ticket sale breaks founder's limiting belief; family resistance dissolves when you prove premium pricing works.
rationale: Cluster conflates two distinct claims: (1) personal belief-breaking moment (psychological shift for founder), (2) market signaling effect (price → expectations). Split preserves both loads.

definition: Eulogies surface what truly matters (service + character); writing yours reveals which worries are noise.
rationale: Both members address same clarification mechanism (what gets said at death = what matters in life). Concrete, falsifiable, changes behavior (priority reset).

definition: Judgment dissolves when you map the person's full reinforcement history and constraints; context generates grace.
rationale: Both express mechanism: learning someone's constraints → compassion → judgment dissolves. One member is illustrative example (family story), not separate claim.

All three members fail NODE-QUALITY rubric A2 (feature-list claim) and A7 (self-referential). 'Recruitment Framework' is definitional stub; 'hire experienced academic sales rep' is tactic without mechanism; 'need to double headcount' is status update, not load-bearing claim.

definition: Acquiring small businesses assumes easy scaling, usually wrong; founder-dependent operations don't scale when owner attention divides.
rationale: Both members identify same failure mode: acquisition thesis assumes scalability but founders create bottleneck. Mechanism (founder dependency) and falsifiability (does it scale?) are clear.

splits.definition: Profit-share structure aligns GM incentives to location performance and enables founder withdrawal; operationalizes scaling out of founder.

definition: Founder attention directly drives profitability; margins collapse when attention splits across locations.
rationale: Two distinct claims: (1) founder magic explains variance (diagnostic), (2) profit-share solves it (prescriptive). Split clarifies mechanism vs. mitigation.

definition: When customers touch 5-10 channels before buying, determining which channel deserves credit becomes ambiguous; longer cycles amplify the problem.
rationale: All three restate same problem: multi-touch attribution is unsolved. Load-bearing: identifying true channel ROI is impossible without solution, changes budget allocation decisions.

canonical_name: Paid Ads vs. Organic: Complementary Not Exclusive
aliases: Paid Ads vs. Organic Growth Foundation

splits.canonical_name: Hybrid Market Entry Accelerates Time to Profitability
splits.definition: Entering new markets: run $100-200/day ads (fast path) parallel to organic playbook (guaranteed path); neither alone reaches profitability on required timeline.
Sell high-ticket offers generating immediate profit; reinvest all profits into ads. Cash flow covers acquisition costs, removing ad budget constraint.

definition: Paid ads (3-5x short-term lift) and organic content (long-term compounding base) are both required over 10-year horizon; neither replaces the other.
rationale: Centroid claim about complementarity is load-bearing; other three members are distinct applications (market entry timing, multi-channel sequencing, unit economics). Split preserves each mechanism.

splits.canonical_name: Guided Menu Upsell Increases Attach Rate
splits.definition: Show upsells by prescribing what customer needs, offering A/B choice, enabling easy payment; increases conversion vs. open menu.
Done-for-you reduces friction but requires operationalization burden; choose pure acquisition play if operationalization costs exceed margin gain.

definition: Customers don't buy lifestyle change directly; they buy defined endpoint, discover need for continuity, then upsell naturally.
rationale: Centroid frames core insight (positioning + discovery path); Menu Upsell is tactic within that (guided prescription), Turnkey is separate positioning decision (operationalization vs. acquisition). Each is load-bearing.

definition: Secondary touchpoints (events, content, milestones) accumulate over 30 days; continue all channels after identifying primary, as cumulative touch count increases conversion.
rationale: All three express same principle: single touchpoint insufficient; cumulative effect (multiple channels, multiple timestamps) drives conversion. Proposed canonical too product-focused; reframe as mechanism.

definition: Burnout is a capacity problem, not a breaking point; work capacity grows with volume if you avoid injury.
rationale: Both reframe burnout: not failure but under-developed capacity. Mechanism (adaptation under load) is falsifiable. Requires operationalization (how to measure capacity vs. breaking point).

splits.definition: Customers fund their purchase upfront; refund contingent on hitting goals or completing actions. Dual accountability (client + operator) and skin-in-game increase completion.
Community-driven support with refund conditions; peer accountability supplements financial accountability.

definition: Reverse buyer risk (you work free until result, or customer gets money back) increases conversions 2-4x; most powerful for service guarantees.
rationale: Centroid is principle (risk reversal = conversion lift); other members are structural variations (who bears risk, what triggers refund, community vs. solo). Each variation is distinct operationally.
